q: What is the star at the centre of the Solar System?
exp: The Sun holds 99.8% of the Solar System's mass.

q: How many planets are in the Solar System?
exp: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.

q: Which planet do we live on?
exp: Earth is the only planet known to have life.

q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
exp: Mars is red because of iron oxide (rust) in its soil.

q: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
exp: Over 1,300 Earths would fit inside Jupiter.

q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
exp: Mercury takes only 88 days to orbit the Sun.

q: Which object orbits the Earth and lights up the night?
exp: The Moon has no light of its own — it reflects the Sun's light.

q: Which planet is famous for its rings?
exp: Saturn's rings are made mostly of ice and rock.

q: What do we call our group of planets and the Sun?
exp: The Solar System lies within the Milky Way galaxy.

q: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
exp: It happened during the Apollo 11 mission in 1969.

q: What is the name of the galaxy we live in?
exp: The Milky Way has over 100 billion stars.
Earth is slightly flattened at the poles — a 'spheroid'.
